author: Diego Sandoval
title: Francisco Lindor (calf) takes live at-bats
desc: Francisco Lindor took live at-bats against a Mets minor-league pitcher on Friday, according to New York manager Carlos Mendoza.
impact: Lindor also simulated innings on defense but did not run the bases as he continues to progress from his calf injury. The shortstop has been out since April 23rd, but he seems to be inching towards a possible return in late June.

author: Diego Sandoval
title: Blaze Jordan called up by Cardinals, set for MLB debut Friday
desc: Blaze Jordan was called up by the St. Louis Cardinals and will make his MLB debut on Friday.
impact: Jordan, the No. 25 prospect for the Cardinals, will bat eighth and start at third base on Friday against the Twins. The 23-year-old has slashed .313/.374/.548 with 11 home runs and 35 RBI in 57 games at the Triple-A level this season. He is a player to keep tabs on in fantasy to see how he adjusts to MLB pitching.

author: Diego Sandoval
title: Blake Snell (elbow) continuing throwing progression
desc: Blake Snell is continuing his throwing progression as he works his way back from an elbow injury, according to Dodgers manager Dave Roberts.
impact: Snell has played catch several times in the past week, and the next step will likely be him throwing off a mound. The veteran lefty has thrown just three innings this season due to elbow and shoulder injuries. A timetable for his return remains unclear.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Kutter Crawford (elbow) sent on rehab assignment
desc: The Red Sox sent Kutter Crawford on a rehab assignment to Florida to continue his rehab.
impact: Crawford is working his way back from  irritation in his right flexor tendon. He could return from the injured list by the end of June.

author: Diego Sandoval
title: Tyler Glasnow (back) could start playing catch soon
desc: Tyler Glasnow could start playing catch soon as he continues to recover from a back injury, according to Dodgers manager Dave Roberts.
impact: Glasnow has been on the IL since May 8th with lower back spasms and suffered a setback in late May that forced him to shut down his throwing. However, it looks like the right-hander could ramp up a throwing program soon. An exact timetable for return remains unknown.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Tanner Houck (elbow) sent on rehab assignment
desc: The Red Sox sent Tanner Houck on a rehab assignment to Florida to continue his rehab.
impact: Houck is working his way back from a hybrid reconstruction of the right ulnar collateral ligament with flexor tendon repair in his right elbow. He remains without a clear timetable to return.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Johan Oviedo (elbow) sent on rehab assignment
desc: The Red Sox sent Johan Oviedo on a rehab assignment to Florida to continue his rehab.
impact: Oviedo is working his way back from a flexor strain. He could return from the injured list by the end of June.

author: Diego Sandoval
title: Tommy Edman (ankle) on track to be activated Tuesday
desc: Tommy Edman is on track to be activated from the injured list on Tuesday, according to Dodgers manager Dave Roberts.
impact: Edman has been on the shelf for the entirety of the season following offseason ankle surgery, but he seems to be on the brink of a return. The utilityman will likely be shifted around the diamond but should see consistent opportunities in the potent LA offense. Edman slashed .225/.274/.382 with 13 homers in 97 games last season.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Ben Joyce (shoulder) throws off mound Friday
desc: Angels manager Kurt Suzuki said Ben Joyce threw off a mound in Arizona on Friday.
impact: Joyce threw 10 pitches, all of which were fastballs. It is a step in the right direction for him as he still remains without a clear timetable to return.

author: Chase Davis
title: Michael Wacha tagged for four runs in tough loss at Texas
desc: Michael Wacha allowed four earned runs on nine hits and one walk while striking out two over seven innings in Thursday's 4-2 loss to Texas.
impact: After the loss, Wacha now has a 3.58 ERA and 1.16 WHIP with a 4-5 record across 14 starts. While he went the distance, he failed to log a quality start for the third straight outing. He'll look to get back on track in a tough matchup next time out against the Nationals.

author: Chase Davis
title: Lars Nootbaar goes yard but Cards come up short vs. Mets
desc: Lars Nootbaar went 2-for-4 with a home run, a double, one run scored and one RBI.
impact: After Thursday's loss, Nootbaar is batting .227 with two homers, five RBI, three runs scored and a .387 wOBA across six games. Despite the low batting average early on, he's got an impressive 96 mph average exit velocity. There's been a lot of hype around him for the last couple of years, but he's failed to stay healthy, and that's going to be the concern going forward.

author: Chase Davis
title: Jimmy Crooks III goes deep, but Cards come up short vs.
desc: Jimmy Crooks III went 1-for-4 in Thursday's 5-4 loss to the Mets with a home run, one run scored and two RBI while striking out once.
impact: This was Crooks' first homer of the season as his batting average sits at .182 through his first seven games. He's started only six of 12 games since being recalled from Triple-A.

author: Chase Davis
title: Alec Burleson goes deep again as Cards fall
desc: Alec Burleson went 1-for-4 in loss to the Mets with a home run, a run scored, and one RBI.
impact: After Thursday's loss to New York, Burleson is batting .290 with 11 home runs, 48 RBI, 35 runs scored and two stolen bases across 65 games. That makes it three straight games with a home run, six straight with a run scored and 10 RBI since the start of June. He's having a terrific season.

author: Diego Sandoval
title: Shohei Ohtani (knee) has imaging come back clean
desc: Shohei Ohtani's imaging of his left knee inflammation on Friday came back clean.
impact: Ohtani is not in the starting lineup for Friday's series opener against the White Sox, and it seems like it may just be to give him an extra day of rest. Dodgers manager Dave Roberts said that Ohtani's injury is "not an IL situation."

author: Chase Davis
title: Hunter Dobbins allows two homers as Cards fall to Mets
desc: Hunter Dobbins allowed three earned runs on seven hits and no walks while striking out five over 4.1 innings in Thursday's loss to the Mets.
impact: After the outing, Dobbins owns a 3.63 ERA and 1.38 WHIP with a win and a save through two starts and two relief appearances. He gave up nine total hard-hit balls, including two homers, and picked up 11 whiffs on 88 pitches. Dobbins really helped the team, though, as they're playing 11 straight days starting from Tuesday. He'll return to the bullpen.
